tools.name: cancelImageCreation.putOperation
tools.description: CancelImageCreation cancels the creation of Image. This operation can only be used on images in a non-terminal state.

tools.name: createComponent.putOperation
tools.description:
Creates a new component that can be used to build, validate, test, and assess your image. The component is based on a YAML document that you specify using exactly one of the following methods:

  • Inline, using the data property in the request body.

  • A URL that points to a YAML document file stored in Amazon S3, using the uri property in the request body.

tools.name: createContainerRecipe.putOperation
tools.description: Creates a new container recipe. Container recipes define how images are configured, tested, and assessed.

tools.name: createDistributionConfiguration.putOperation
tools.description: Creates a new distribution configuration. Distribution configurations define and configure the outputs of your pipeline.

tools.name: createImage.putOperation
tools.description: Creates a new image. This request will create a new image along with all of the configured output resources defined in the distribution configuration. You must specify exactly one recipe for your image, using either a ContainerRecipeArn or an ImageRecipeArn.

tools.name: createImagePipeline.putOperation
tools.description: Creates a new image pipeline. Image pipelines enable you to automate the creation and distribution of images.

tools.name: createImageRecipe.putOperation
tools.description: Creates a new image recipe. Image recipes define how images are configured, tested, and assessed.

tools.name: createInfrastructureConfiguration.putOperation
tools.description: Creates a new infrastructure configuration. An infrastructure configuration defines the environment in which your image will be built and tested.

tools.name: deleteComponentComponentBuildVersionArn.deleteComponent
tools.description: Deletes a component build version.

tools.name: deleteContainerRecipeContainerRecipeArn.deleteContainerRecipe
tools.description: Deletes a container recipe.

tools.name: deleteDistributionConfigurationDistributionConfigurationArn.deleteDistributionConfiguration
tools.description: Deletes a distribution configuration.

tools.name: deleteImageImageBuildVersionArn.deleteImage
tools.description:
Deletes an Image Builder image resource. This does not delete any EC2 AMIs or ECR container images that are created during the image build process. You must clean those up separately, using the appropriate Amazon EC2 or Amazon ECR console actions, or API or CLI commands.

  • To deregister an EC2 Linux AMI, see Deregister your Linux AMI in the Amazon EC2 User Guide .

  • To deregister an EC2 Windows AMI, see Deregister your Windows AMI in the Amazon EC2 Windows Guide .

  • To delete a container image from Amazon ECR, see Deleting an image in the Amazon ECR User Guide.

tools.name: deleteImagePipelineImagePipelineArn.deleteImagePipeline
tools.description: Deletes an image pipeline.

tools.name: deleteImageRecipeImageRecipeArn.deleteImageRecipe
tools.description: Deletes an image recipe.

tools.name: deleteInfrastructureConfigurationInfrastructureConfigurationArn.deleteInfrastructureConfiguration
tools.description: Deletes an infrastructure configuration.

tools.name: getComponentComponentBuildVersionArn.getComponent
tools.description: Gets a component object.

tools.name: getComponentPolicyComponentArn.getComponentPolicy
tools.description: Gets a component policy.

tools.name: getContainerRecipeContainerRecipeArn.getContainerRecipe
tools.description: Retrieves a container recipe.

tools.name: getContainerRecipePolicyContainerRecipeArn.getContainerRecipePolicy
tools.description: Retrieves the policy for a container recipe.

tools.name: getDistributionConfigurationDistributionConfigurationArn.getDistributionConfiguration
tools.description: Gets a distribution configuration.

tools.name: getImageImageBuildVersionArn.getImage
tools.description: Gets an image.

tools.name: getImagePipelineImagePipelineArn.getImagePipeline
tools.description: Gets an image pipeline.

tools.name: getImagePolicyImageArn.getImagePolicy
tools.description: Gets an image policy.

tools.name: getImageRecipeImageRecipeArn.getImageRecipe
tools.description: Gets an image recipe.

tools.name: getImageRecipePolicyImageRecipeArn.getImageRecipePolicy
tools.description: Gets an image recipe policy.

tools.name: getInfrastructureConfigurationInfrastructureConfigurationArn.getInfrastructureConfiguration
tools.description: Gets an infrastructure configuration.

tools.name: getWorkflowExecutionWorkflowExecutionId.getWorkflowExecution
tools.description: Get the runtime information that was logged for a specific runtime instance of the workflow.

tools.name: getWorkflowStepExecutionStepExecutionId.getWorkflowStepExecution
tools.description: Get the runtime information that was logged for a specific runtime instance of the workflow step.

tools.name: importComponent.putOperation
tools.description: Imports a component and transforms its data into a component document.

tools.name: importVmImage.putOperation
tools.description:
When you export your virtual machine (VM) from its virtualization environment, that process creates a set of one or more disk container files that act as snapshots of your VM’s environment, settings, and data. The Amazon EC2 API ImportImage action uses those files to import your VM and create an AMI. To import using the CLI command, see import-image

You can reference the task ID from the VM import to pull in the AMI that the import created as the base image for your Image Builder recipe.

tools.name: listComponentBuildVersions.postOperation
tools.description:
Returns the list of component build versions for the specified semantic version.

The semantic version has four nodes: <major>.<minor>.<patch>/<build>. You can assign values for the first three, and can filter on all of them.

Filtering: With semantic versioning, you have the flexibility to use wildcards (x) to specify the most recent versions or nodes when selecting the base image or components for your recipe. When you use a wildcard in any node, all nodes to the right of the first wildcard must also be wildcards.

tools.name: listComponents.postOperation
tools.description:
Returns the list of components that can be filtered by name, or by using the listed filters to streamline results. Newly created components can take up to two minutes to appear in the ListComponents API Results.

The semantic version has four nodes: <major>.<minor>.<patch>/<build>. You can assign values for the first three, and can filter on all of them.

Filtering: With semantic versioning, you have the flexibility to use wildcards (x) to specify the most recent versions or nodes when selecting the base image or components for your recipe. When you use a wildcard in any node, all nodes to the right of the first wildcard must also be wildcards.

tools.name: listContainerRecipes.postOperation
tools.description: Returns a list of container recipes.

tools.name: listDistributionConfigurations.postOperation
tools.description: Returns a list of distribution configurations.

tools.name: listImageBuildVersions.postOperation
tools.description: Returns a list of image build versions.

tools.name: listImagePackages.postOperation
tools.description: List the Packages that are associated with an Image Build Version, as determined by Amazon Web Services Systems Manager Inventory at build time.

tools.name: listImagePipelineImages.postOperation
tools.description: Returns a list of images created by the specified pipeline.

tools.name: listImagePipelines.postOperation
tools.description: Returns a list of image pipelines.

tools.name: listImageRecipes.postOperation
tools.description: Returns a list of image recipes.

tools.name: listImages.postOperation
tools.description: Returns the list of images that you have access to. Newly created images can take up to two minutes to appear in the ListImages API Results.

tools.name: listImageScanFindingAggregations.postOperation
tools.description:
Returns a list of image scan aggregations for your account. You can filter by the type of key that Image Builder uses to group results. For example, if you want to get a list of findings by severity level for one of your pipelines, you might specify your pipeline with the imagePipelineArn filter. If you don't specify a filter, Image Builder returns an aggregation for your account.

To streamline results, you can use the following filters in your request:

  • accountId

  • imageBuildVersionArn

  • imagePipelineArn

  • vulnerabilityId

tools.name: listImageScanFindings.postOperation
tools.description: Returns a list of image scan findings for your account.

tools.name: listInfrastructureConfigurations.postOperation
tools.description: Returns a list of infrastructure configurations.

tools.name: listWorkflowExecutions.postOperation
tools.description: Returns a list of workflow runtime instance metadata objects for a specific image build version.

tools.name: listWorkflowStepExecutions.postOperation
tools.description: Shows runtime data for each step in a runtime instance of the workflow that you specify in the request.

tools.name: putComponentPolicy.putOperation
tools.description: Applies a policy to a component. We recommend that you call the RAM API CreateResourceShare to share resources. If you call the Image Builder API PutComponentPolicy, you must also call the RAM API PromoteResourceShareCreatedFromPolicy in order for the resource to be visible to all principals with whom the resource is shared.

tools.name: putContainerRecipePolicy.putOperation
tools.description: Applies a policy to a container image. We recommend that you call the RAM API CreateResourceShare (https://docs.aws.amazon.com//ram/latest/APIReference/API_CreateResourceShare.html) to share resources. If you call the Image Builder API PutContainerImagePolicy, you must also call the RAM API PromoteResourceShareCreatedFromPolicy (https://docs.aws.amazon.com//ram/latest/APIReference/API_PromoteResourceShareCreatedFromPolicy.html) in order for the resource to be visible to all principals with whom the resource is shared.

tools.name: putImagePolicy.putOperation
tools.description: Applies a policy to an image. We recommend that you call the RAM API CreateResourceShare to share resources. If you call the Image Builder API PutImagePolicy, you must also call the RAM API PromoteResourceShareCreatedFromPolicy in order for the resource to be visible to all principals with whom the resource is shared.

tools.name: putImageRecipePolicy.putOperation
tools.description: Applies a policy to an image recipe. We recommend that you call the RAM API CreateResourceShare to share resources. If you call the Image Builder API PutImageRecipePolicy, you must also call the RAM API PromoteResourceShareCreatedFromPolicy in order for the resource to be visible to all principals with whom the resource is shared.

tools.name: startImagePipelineExecution.putOperation
tools.description: Manually triggers a pipeline to create an image.

tools.name: tags.listTagsForResource
tools.description: Returns the list of tags for the specified resource.

tools.name: tags.tagResource
tools.description: Adds a tag to a resource.

tools.name: tags.untagResource
tools.description: Removes a tag from a resource.

tools.name: updateDistributionConfiguration.putOperation
tools.description: Updates a new distribution configuration. Distribution configurations define and configure the outputs of your pipeline.

tools.name: updateImagePipeline.putOperation
tools.description:
Updates an image pipeline. Image pipelines enable you to automate the creation and distribution of images.

UpdateImagePipeline does not support selective updates for the pipeline. You must specify all of the required properties in the update request, not just the properties that have changed.

tools.name: updateInfrastructureConfiguration.putOperation
tools.description: Updates a new infrastructure configuration. An infrastructure configuration defines the environment in which your image will be built and tested.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
